name: Nauruan
notes: Nauruan (Dorerin Naoero), a Micronesian (Austronesian) language, national language of Nauru. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Latin alphabet. Although a 1938 reform (Detudamo's committee) moved the spelling toward European conventions and gave it around 30 letters, the reform was only partially adopted: ⟨ã⟩ and ⟨ñ⟩ are now rarely used (replaced by e and ng), and conservative spellings persist in names (Baiti, Ijuw), so the orthography is not reliably phonemic. A correct spec would also need the velarised/palatalised consonant series, the large vowel inventory with reduction to schwa, and the /t d/ → [tʃ dʒ] palatalisation before high front vowels. Input is assumed to be the standard Latin Nauruan orthography.
